2026-04-08T15:45:20.0333673Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-08T15:45:20.0334112Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-08T15:45:20.0334510Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-08T15:45:20.0334836Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). 2026-04-08T15:48:24.8626301Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T15:46:24.481535847Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57937,\"title\":\"[ComputeSchedule] Docs: Add code snippets for VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex and wire README examples\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57188) by `@azure-sdk` (co-authored by `@Asifmammadov89`)\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57188 added support for the `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` API operation (introduced in API version `2026-03-01-preview`). This operation enables on-demand flexible VM provisioning with Spot or regular priority, configurable VM size profiles, OS type, and allocation strategy. The PR added scenario tests for this operation but no README documentation or test-backed code snippets.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples** section only links to a generic management samples page — it has no package-specific code snippets for any of the ComputeSchedule operations\\n- The new `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` operation has no sample code or documentation showing how to use it (required parameters, priority type enum, VM size profiles, allocation strategy, inline network config)\\n- None of the existing sample files in `tests/Samples/` use `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers, so **no samples are wired up** to appear in the README via the snippet injection system\\n- The README already mentions `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` in the intro but does not demonstrate usage with a code example\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\nReplace the current minimal Examples section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith an expanded section that includes placeholder blocks for the snippet tool to inject:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n\\n### Execute an immediate VM start\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex)\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — Execute start\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nWrap an existing `VirtualMachinesExecuteStart` test method body with a named region (or add a new dedicated sample method):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task ExecuteStart_Sample()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n    SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n    var resources = new ExecuteResourceOperationContent(\\n        new ResourceOperationDetails(\\n            new UserRequestRetryPolicy(RetryCount.One, RetryWindowInMinutes.Thirty),\\n            new List { new ResourceId(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}\\u0026#34;) }),\\n        new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString()));\\n\\n    Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteStartAsync(\\n        AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n        resources);\\n\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Operation result: {response.Value.Type}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Under the `### Execute an immediate VM start` heading added in Step 1.\\n**Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart`\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### Snippet 2 — ExecuteCreateFlex\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new sample method:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task ExecuteCreateFlex_Sample()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n    SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n    var flexRequest = new ExecuteCreateFlexContent(\\n        new CreateFlexDetails(\\n            new List\\n            {\\n                new VmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;) { Rank = 1 },\\n                new VmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D4s_v3\\u0026#34;) { Rank = 2 }\\n            },\\n            OSType.Windows,\\n            AllocationStrategy.Prioritized,\\n            new InlineNetworkInterfaceConfiguration(\\n                new SubnetConfiguration(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnet}/subnets/{subnet}\\u0026#34;))),\\n        new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString()))\\n    {\\n        PriorityType = PriorityType.Spot\\n    };\\n\\n    Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlexAsync(\\n        AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n        flexRequest);\\n\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created VMs: {response.Value.Results.Count}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Under the `### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex)` heading added in Step 1.\\n**Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections. The **Examples** section (gap #1 above) needs to be fleshed out with actual snippet placeholders as described above. All other sections are present but reference to `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` should remain in the intro.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n\\n### Execute an immediate VM start\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex)\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — Execute start\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nWrap an existing `VirtualMachinesExecuteStart` test method body with a named region (or add a new dedicated sample method):\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task ExecuteStart_Sample()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n    SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n    var resources = new ExecuteResourceOperationContent(\\n        new ResourceOperationDetails(\\n            new UserRequestRetryPolicy(RetryCount.One, RetryWindowInMinutes.Thirty),\\n            new List { new ResourceId(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}\\u0026#34;) }),\\n        new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString()));\\n\\n    Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteStartAsync(\\n        AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n        resources);\\n\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Operation result: {response.Value.Type}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Under the `### Execute an immediate VM start` heading added in Step 1.\\n**Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteStart`\\n\\n---\\n\\n#### Snippet 2 — ExecuteCreateFlex\\n\\n**Test file:** `sdk/computeschedule/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeSchedule/tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`\\n\\nAdd a new sample method:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic async Task ExecuteCreateFlex_Sample()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex\\n    ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n    SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n    var flexRequest = new ExecuteCreateFlexContent(\\n        new CreateFlexDetails(\\n            new List\\n            {\\n                new VmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D2s_v3\\u0026#34;) { Rank = 1 },\\n                new VmSizeProfile(\\u0026#34;Standard_D4s_v3\\u0026#34;) { Rank = 2 }\\n            },\\n            OSType.Windows,\\n            AllocationStrategy.Prioritized,\\n            new InlineNetworkInterfaceConfiguration(\\n                new SubnetConfiguration(\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnet}/subnets/{subnet}\\u0026#34;))),\\n        new CorrelationId(Guid.NewGuid().ToString()))\\n    {\\n        PriorityType = PriorityType.Spot\\n    };\\n\\n    Response response = await subscription.VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlexAsync(\\n        AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n        flexRequest);\\n\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created VMs: {response.Value.Results.Count}\\u0026#34;);\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README location:** Under the `### Execute flexible VM creation (VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex)` heading added in Step 1.\\n**Snippet name:** `ComputeSchedule_ExecuteCreateFlex`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README All other sections are present but reference to `VirtualMachinesExecuteCreateFlex` should remain in the intro.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order.
